title: Path compression

publication.changeSummary: Language-neutral DSA draft with explicit C/C++/Java/Python mapping for Path compression; independent algorithm, implementation, pedagogy, and accessibility review required. Beginner-quality contract 1.0.0 applied deterministically from existing source-linked draft facts; publication stage and reviewer state are unchanged.

sources.locator: CS2023 Algorithmic Foundations; August 2023; material applicable to Path compression
sources.claim: Path compression rewrites parent links during find so visited nodes point closer or directly to the representative.

canonicalExplanation.summary: Path compression rewrites parent links during find so visited nodes point closer or directly to the representative.

sections.heading: Start here
sections.body: Start with this case: A recursive find assigns parent[x] to the representative returned for its current parent. Your goal is to implement path compression and compare operation traces before and after optimization. Work through the example one step at a time, then use the boundary to check your answer.

sections.heading: Before you begin
sections.body: Review these declared prerequisites first: Tree terminology and representation; Array representation and operations; Union by rank or size; Time, space and asymptotic growth. If one is unfamiliar, revisit it before continuing.

sections.heading: Shared concept and invariant
sections.body: Path compression rewrites parent links during find so visited nodes point closer or directly to the representative. This claim is assessed independently from syntax so conceptual evidence can transfer.

sections.heading: C, C++, Java, and Python implementation map
sections.body: In C, represent adjacency and disjoint sets with explicit buffers and ownership; C++ uses vector-based lists and standard priority structures; Java uses typed collections and careful object equality; Python uses list/dict/set and heapq, with the same graph or partition invariant and complexity target.

sections.heading: Boundary and common failure
sections.body: Compression changes internal parents but not partition membership and combines with union heuristics for inverse-Ackermann amortized cost. The draft therefore rejects: Path compression changes which elements belong to each set.

includes: Shorten find paths while preserving component identity and explain its amortized benefit conceptually.
Implement path compression and compare operation traces before and after optimization.

In C, represent adjacency and disjoint sets with explicit buffers and ownership; C++ uses vector-based lists and standard priority structures; Java uses typed collections and careful object equality; Python uses list/dict/set and heapq, with the same graph or partition invariant and complexity target.
excludes: Compression changes internal parents but not partition membership and combines with union heuristics for inverse-Ackermann amortized cost.

outcomes: Implement path compression and compare operation traces before and after optimization.

Step 2 — Map storage, library, equality, bounds, ownership, and error rules using: In C, represent adjacency and disjoint sets with explicit buffers and ownership; C++ uses vector-based lists and standard priority structures; Java uses typed collections and careful object equality; Python uses list/dict/set and heapq, with the same graph or partition invariant and complexity target.

Step 3 — Retest this boundary in the selected implementation: Compression changes internal parents but not partition membership and combines with union heuristics for inverse-Ackermann amortized cost.
